On April 15, 1991 supervisor Charles Santana of District 2, Alameda
Counly, passed away. At the time of his death he left approximately
thirti 
thousand dollars in his "Santana For Supervisor' Committee
account.
As treasurer of the committee that holds these funds - Santana For
Supervisor - I would like some advice as to procedures regarding
these funds.
My questions are as follows:
I
2
3
4
5
6
Since these funds are no longer to be used to seek reeletion,
what do they become?
How do we disperse these funds?
Who has the responsibility for dispersing these funds?
What are the guidelines we should follow for dispersing
these funds ?
Who makes the decisions regarding the lawful dispersion
of these funds ?
What are the obligations of the Treasurer regarding this
matte r ?
7l Does Lhe Estate Attorney representing the estate of the
- 1at" Supervisdr have any authority to advise or influence
the Treasurer of thi.s committee?
8) Can the Treasurer of this committee resign his position?
9i Would the resignation of the Treasurer relieve him from
responsibility for the eventual dispersion of the funds?
I appreciate your help in this matter, and thank you in advance for
your assistance.
SincerelY,
,aa*/*/
Anthony Mastrantonio
Treasuier, Santana For SuPervisor
cc: Mr. Ted Prim, Attorney General office

sections.question: Since these funds are no longer to be used to seek reeletion, what do they become? How do we disperse these funds? Who has the responsibility for dispersing these funds? What are the guidelines we should follow for dispersing these funds? Who makes the decisions regarding the lawful dispersion of these funds? What are the obligations of the Treasurer regarding this matter? Does the Estate Attorney representing the estate of the late Supervisor have any authority to advise or influence the Treasurer of this committee? Can the Treasurer of this committee resign his position? Would the resignation of the Treasurer relieve him from responsibility for the eventual dispersion of the funds?
sections.question_synthetic: The treasurer of a deceased supervisor's campaign committee seeks guidance on the proper procedures for dispersing approximately thirty thousand dollars remaining in the committee's account, including questions about fund disposition, treasurer responsibilities, the role of the estate attorney, and treasurer resignation implications.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion/answer section is present. Nine numbered questions are clearly presented. Document is dated December 20, 1991.

What are the obligations of the Treasurer regarding this matter? Does the Estate Attorney representing the estate of the late Supervisor have any authority to advise or influence the Treasurer of this committee? Can the Treasurer of this committee resign his position? Would the resignation of the Treasurer relieve him from responsibility for the eventual dispersion of the funds?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from Anthony Mastrantonio, treasurer of the Santana For Supervisor Committee, asking the FPPC for advice on how to handle campaign funds remaining after Supervisor Charles Santana's death in April 1991.
